title: Locomotive Engineers
description: Drive electric, diesel-electric, steam, or gas-turbine-electric locomotives to transport passengers or freight. Interpret train orders, electronic or manual signals, and railroad rules and regulations.

tasks.text: Interpret train orders, signals, or railroad rules and regulations that govern the operation of locomotives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with conductors or traffic control center personnel via radiophones to issue or receive information concerning stops, delays, or oncoming trains.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Receive starting signals from conductors and use controls such as throttles or air brakes to drive electric, diesel-electric, steam, or gas turbine-electric locomotives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor gauges or meters that measure speed, amperage, battery charge, or air pressure in brake lines or in main reservoirs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Call out train signals to assistants to verify meanings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate locomotives to transport freight or passengers between stations or to assemble or disassemble trains within rail yards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check to ensure that brake examination tests are conducted at shunting stations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Respond to emergency conditions or breakdowns, following applicable safety procedures and rules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect locomotives to verify adequate fuel, sand, water, or other supplies before each run or to check for mechanical problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inspect locomotives after runs to detect damaged or defective equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prepare reports regarding any problems encountered, such as accidents, signaling problems, unscheduled stops, or delays.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check to ensure that documentation, such as procedure manuals or logbooks, are in the driver's cab and available for staff use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor train loading procedures to ensure that freight or rolling stock are loaded or unloaded without damage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
